researched_category.notes: Linear is a modern issue tracking and project planning tool for software teams, built on a GraphQL API.

one_liner.value: Linear is a purpose-built project management and issue tracking platform for software teams, offering a GraphQL API and official MCP server for AI agent integration.

methods.notes: Linear supports OAuth2 (recommended for multi-user apps) and personal API keys (for personal scripts). The MCP server uses OAuth 2.1 with dynamic client registration.

access.notes: Free plan includes 'API and webhook access' and 'MCP access'. API keys can be self-generated from Settings > Account > Security & Access. No special approval needed for API access.

summary.value: Linear's public GraphQL API (https://api.linear.app/graphql) provides full CRUD access to issues, projects, cycles, teams, users, comments, labels, attachments, roadmaps, and more. It supports pagination, filtering, webhooks, file uploads, and introspection. Rate limits: 5,000 requests/hour with API key, 600/hour unauthenticated. Official TypeScript SDK (@linear/sdk) available.

agent_interface.notes: Linear hosts an official remote MCP server at https://mcp.linear.app/mcp supporting Streamable HTTP transport with OAuth 2.1. It provides tools for issues, projects, teams, cycles, comments, and documents. Compatible with Claude, Cursor, Codex, VS Code, Windsurf, Zed, and others. Composio also provides a Linear toolkit with 32 tools and 3 triggers.

rationale.value: Linear has everything needed for an agent toolkit today: a well-documented public GraphQL API, self-serve API keys on the free plan, OAuth2 for multi-user apps, an official hosted MCP server, a TypeScript SDK, webhooks for event-driven workflows, and comprehensive developer documentation including agent interaction guidelines. No gates or paid plans are required for basic API access.

s1.quote: Learn about building apps and integrations for Linear using the GraphQL API and TypeScript SDK.

s2.quote: Linear's public API is built using GraphQL. It's the same API we use internally for developing our applications.

s3.quote: Free: $0 - Unlimited members, 2 teams, 250 issues, Agent platform, Linear Agent

s4.quote: The Linear API supports personal API keys and OAuth2 authentication.

s5.quote: Linear supports OAuth2 authentication, which is recommended if you're building applications to integrate with Linear.

s6.quote: Free: $0 per user/month - API and webhook access, MCP access

s7.quote: You own your data in Linear and our GraphQL API lets you query that data. Admins and permitted Members can create personal API keys from Settings > Account > Security & Access.

s9.quote: When authenticated using an API key you can make up to 5,000 requests per hour.

s10.quote: Install the Linear SDK to use strongly typed models and operations

s11.quote: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) server provides a standardized interface that allows any compatible AI model or agent to access your Linear data in a simple and secure way.

s12.quote: Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Linear MCP or direct API... 32 Tools 3 Triggers

s13.quote: Your AI models and agents can use our official MCP server to access your Linear data in a simple and secure way.

s14.quote: This guide describes how to best integrate an AI agent into Linear. It includes implementation guidelines on how to design an experience that feels native to Linear's workflows.
